Did you know wild dogs don't all bite the same? From tiny desert foxes to powerful wolves, bite force can vary a lot in the wild. In this video, we compare the bite strength of [music] wild canids, including foxes, jackals, and wolves, using PSI, a measure of how strong each bite really is. You'll see the weakest hunters and the most powerful predators. [music] Watch till the end to find out which wild canid has the strongest bite. >> [music] [music]

What if humans never left Africa? Around 70,000 years ago, modern humans were ready to [music] explore the world, but something changed. The Sahara grew into a massive deadly desert, blocking the north. The Arabian lands turned into a [music] scorching lifeless zone, stopping movement into Asia. Even coastal routes became [music] unstable, flooded, or barren. And inside Africa, life was good. Rich rivers, abundant [music] wildlife, and perfect conditions to survive and grow. With no safe path out [music] and no reason to leave, humanity stayed. But what happens next? How would civilizations rise, evolve, and eventually discover the world? Watch till [music] the end to see how history changes. >> [music] [music]

What if humans were not the only intelligent species on Earth? Imagine a world where Neanderthals never went [music] extinct and lived alongside us for thousands of years. Two human species >> [music] >> sharing the same planet, competing, cooperating, and evolving together. Would history be the same? Would we build civilizations together or fight for survival? In this video, we explore a complete alternate timeline from the ice age to the future, showing how the world could have changed if both species existed side by side. Watch till the end to see how this story ends. >> [music] [music]

Have you ever wondered how big wildcats really are? From incredibly small species that can fit in your hands to powerful giants that rule the wild.
